sentences.text: Hey, John.
Hi, Nathan, can you hear me?
Yes, I can, can you hear me?
Okay, good, I'm just having trouble getting in, my apologies.
All good, all good.
How's your day going so far?
Alright, just catching up on a bunch of emails, how's yours?
Yeah, my day's going good.
We're, it's hump day, so we're almost to the weekend.
Good, good, good.
Yep, we're almost there.
We are.
You got any big plans for this coming weekend?
… No, not really, just… taking care of horses.
I gotcha.
There you go, that's better.
Yeah, how many horses do you have?
Wow, okay.
Yeah, four horses.
My husband's a team roper, so….
Okay.
Oh yeah, a lot of our… we do a lot of horse stuff.
Yeah, I was about to say, you go riding a lot?
Yeah, yeah, yeah, we try to.
At least, that's fun for me.
Every weekend, I like to ride.
Oh, yeah.
So, yeah.
I'm sure it's kind of relaxing, right?
You ride out, you see the sunsets, and….
Yeah, yeah, the family comes out, everyone loves to ride.
Oh, yeah.
I've been once or twice, and I really enjoyed it.
I'm in… I'm in Nashville, so I mean, like, it's… it's horse country here, too, but nothing like it's in Texas or in, like, Kentucky.
Yeah.
But, but yeah, it was a lot of fun.
Yeah.
You don't realize how big a horse is until you stand up next to it.
I know, right?
They're very intimidating, but … Yeah, for a lot of people.
Yeah.
They are, but they're such, like, gentle beasts at the same time.
Yeah, they're like… they're like dogs.
They're smart, they can get out of their stalls and everything, they know how to open them, I don't know how they do it.
They're… they test your patience.
We also have goats and lambs, they're pretty smart, too.
Okay, do you live on… I'm assuming you live on a farm?
Yeah.
16 acres.
Okay.
We're about an hour out from Houston, so I drive in every morning.
Nice, nice.
It's always nice having land.
I'm sitting on.
25 acres right now.
Oh.
25, that's nice.
Yeah, so quite smaller than, what you're sitting on, but….
Yeah, well, I've never been to Nashville, but I know we have some properties over there.
They were gonna send me there one week.
I ended up not going, but….
It's a good time.
I think you'd really enjoy it once you were here.
Yeah.
I mean, it's… I'd say it's very similar to Texas.
It's… it probably gives more of, like, the Austin vibe, though.
Probably, I guess the music scene.
I remember y'all don't wear cowboy hats over there, either.
They make fun of the Texas people coming in.
No, people are wearing cowboy hats and cowgirl hats and boots.
Yes.
Okay, okay.
You wouldn't be alone if you wore one.
Alright, good to know.
So, I saw on LinkedIn, you've been at Tarantino now for, what, like, 4 to 6 months?
Oh, well, that's actually… I've been… this position… I've been with the company for almost 3 years.
That's right, that is what it says.
Yeah, yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, so, yeah, I started out with the digital marketing when I went to corporate.
Well, I did leasing, and then kind of worked my way up to digital marketing, and now we're just doing the marketing umbrella, then visiting the properties on-site.
But what… I guess you kind of caught me at the right time, because I have a lot of regionals that were questioning one of our main advertising, sources, which is Apartments.
com.
Okay.

And, I know we pay a lot for them, so I just wanted to see what you could offer us as far as more online visibility, how the leads… how y'all Track the leads, how you'll get them, or, you know, exactly.
Yeah, for sure, I'm happy to explain all that to you.
I have a couple of questions about your portfolio first, just to make sure I have all my ducks in a row.
But I was trying to… how many properties do you have now?
I don't even know… I don't know if they're even listed on our website.
So we did get a lot of receiverships.
Okay.
And that's probably the main source, the main bulk of our portfolio, and receiverships are assigned to us by a court, right?
Before the apartments go into foreclosure.
So, a judge will assign us a receivership.
It happens all the time.
We get property, you know, we might have one tomorrow that we don't know we're getting.
So, yeah, they come in and out.
all the time, so I… I really do not know how many we have on, as of right now, but I know it's over 100.
Okay, and you're… it looked like… so, it looked like you had everything from, like, student to senior, all the way to just, like, regular conventional A to C class, … Right.
Right, and I'm probably more on the conventional side, just so you know.
No, that's… and that's perfect.
Are those properties all solely in Texas, or are they also in other states?
All throughout.
So, I was in Arkansas for almost 3 weeks last month.
So… so, yeah, all over.
Okay, perfect.
That works.
So, with… with a receivership, I've only heard that term, I think, like, 2 other times.
Yeah.
So does that… do you basically ask, like, a third-party property management company for them then, or do they assign them to you and then you end up owning them?
They assign it to us, and then whatever happens, should it go through auction, sell, things like that, the bank, it becomes… Owned, again, by the bank.
They take over it, so we're in there, kind of, cleaning it up.
Sometimes we're there until they figure out what's going on through the court process.
I mean, it's just different.
you know, different phases of when we get in, you know?
Sometimes the owner will come back and somehow buy it out, I don't know.
So, you know, it's just, it's new to me also, as far as the receiverships.
I've never heard about that until I was with Tarantino.
Okay.
You're probably one of the largest, though, as far as taking the receiverships.
So, and that's why I was like, how many properties do you have?
Because on the website, all I saw was… it only showed me the amount for senior living, but then I went.
Aww.
And I was like, well, it looks like there's, like, over 90-ish, almost, so….
Yeah, yeah, yeah, there's… we have quite a few.
I'll have to look at our website, I didn't know we only had Apartments.
com.
Maybe because the conventional side, we just, you know.
We're… managing, and then, I mean, they just… it moves really fast with the receiverships.
Sometimes we hold them for years, so….
you've got to be on your toes and ready for anything, which I guess Good job.
Hard and exciting at the same time, at least.
Yeah.
Yeah, we gotta be fast, we do receiverships, it's sign it to us, sometimes we don't know we're flying out that week.
We hop on a plane and show up.
Gotcha, okay.
Aside from Apartments.
com, what do you do right now, advertising-wise?
So we're advertising with Zillow, ApartmentList.
Okay.
… depending on the property, sometimes, we're trying to do, like, organic stuff ourselves, like social media.
We do hire marketing companies.
We work, a lot with Wickly, which also hosts a lot of our websites, and they have a marketing department where they can do… maintain our social medias and, you know, things like that.
We're kind of probably all over.
Okay, gotcha.
But it really depends on the property, the demographic, the….
Yeah.
Yorkie.
Yeah, that's what we're trying to tailor it to.
I mean, it might not be worth us paying $1,500 for the Platinum Plus package if we're only getting one lead, one lease a month, you know?
Might be better to… Put that somewhere else and downgrade that package.
For sure, and that's… and that's actually what I was gonna go into.
So, like, we're working with a couple of big names in your area, like Sunridge.
Okay.
New residential, KPM, Asset Living, Indus, I mean, a ton of companies, and a lot of them are doing exactly what you just said, where they were on the big, large package with Apartments.
com, weren't getting the results, and paying $1,500 to $2,000 a month per property just as.
Yeah.
So, they dropped packages with them, to, like, a lower package, like the gold.
They brought us on, and we got them more exposure than what… what they were getting.
That's interesting.
We have a couple different routes, that we… we take, and that's what makes us so unique.
I'll share my screen and kind of walk you through what we offer.
Okay.
So… so we're an internet listing site, just like in Apartments.
com, first and foremost.
… But a couple of different things help us stand out.
One is we have a massive syndicate network.
So, … I'll dive into that in a second.
And the others, we have, were super cost-effective.
Pennies to the dollar to that Apartments.
com price, but with the way we work, we typically integrate with your property management software.
Are you… it looks like you were using Yardi, is that what you used?
No, one site, but we do probably have a few stragglers that use Entrada and might use Yardi.
Okay, so….
But we're mainly one site.
A real page.
Gotcha.
Are you using their Elise AI as well?
knock.
I'm sorry, I'm not familiar with that.
What is that?
Knock… sometimes.
Oh, yeah, yeah, NOC, yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, we are.
sometimes people are using NOC and Elise AI, which Elise AI is, like, their… leasing AI bot that picks up the conversations and helps schedule tours and things of that nature?
Oh, yeah, like, we'll have thoughts on our page, is that what you're referring to?
I think sometimes….
beliefs and thoughts.
Web pages, too.
Okay.
But if you have NOC, that's… that's the primary one, right?
Yeah.
We integrate with OneSite, we integrate with Knox, so anytime you're updating information on your end, like pricing, availability, pictures, we'll pull it, and we'll… We'll have it.
Thank you.
Okay.
So, we're gonna list your property on our site, and then from there, we'll syndicate it out.
When I say we syndicate it out, we have the… I say the industry's largest network, because we go basically everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
So, that's the only site we really don't go to.
And then what we're doing is we're utilizing AI to make sure we're not duplicating any of your listings.
So, like, you already mentioned how you're using Zillow on some sites, and Lists on some sites.
We'll pick up on that, so we're not pushing to those sites for those specific properties, but then let's say, out of your other 20 properties that aren't using those sites, we can.
push and get you there on those sites.
Okay.
So, that would include Zillow, you're saying?
So, we wouldn't have to contact each little… okay.
Correct, and list.
I'm not here to tell you to leave them by any means.
We usually let their paid subscriptions take precedent, but if you were to decide to leave them down the road, we would pick up and fill in the gaps where they're leaving off.
Oh, no.
So… and that's our… that's our big play, is filling in the gaps, getting your property more exposure on more sites, because apartments in Zillow are big, right?
But if you look at the overall renter audience, that's probably only 40% of the renter market.
So we're getting you on all those other additional sites that you're not utilizing, and then with that, we also have an all-inclusive targeted social ad piece.
So, we're getting your properties on Facebook and Instagram as well, and reaching out to those renters versus waiting for them to come to you.
So, the way those work for us is anytime somebody's going on a search engine, they're saying, show me apartments in Houston, or in Dallas, wherever it may be.
Every time they get on their Facebook and they're scrolling, and you've probably seen this with other things, like, why not.
Hmm.
Or jerseys, or sweatshirts.
when they go and they scroll, we're gonna hit them with a rentable ad that looks exactly like this.
It'll have pictures of your property, details about it, lowest advertised price, if there's a pool, if it's pet-friendly, things to help it stand out.
And then where our ads differ from a lot of other agencies is other agencies do one of two things.
They usually redirect them to their personal site, and at that point, a renter can see every single listing that they're advertising for and doing ads for.
Or, they'll redirect them to a property website.
Which is cool, but then you lose track of, did that person become a lead?
Can it be attributed to that company?
You don't know.

With us, all of our… all of our ads are lead generating, so we're gonna keep them there, we're gonna capture their information, like their names, their emails, their phone numbers, and then we're gonna deliver that directly into your NOC as a guest card.
Okay.
And that's how all of our leads would come in.
We can deliver it directly into NOC, we can deliver it to an on-site leasing email address, both, whichever you prefer.
But y'all are integrated, I'm sorry, with RealPage, right?
Yes.
Okay, yeah, so if we pull this primary advertising report, I guess we could, … you know, advertising source, I guess we would have to… Make sure you're on here, it would.
We are.
We are.
So we fully integrate with RealPage, one site, knock, everything.
So everything that comes in, whether it's from us, our syndicate network, or our social ads, it's all attributed as a rentable lead.
I think in role page, it might pop up as a Bodo.
Okay.
That's our old name.
We changed our name back in, like, 2012.
Oh, what does it pop up as?
a Bodo?
A photo.
A-B-O-D-O?
Okay.
Okay.
But either way, I know we fully integrate with them, so….
Okay, well, this is interesting.
Yeah.
I'll pause there for a second, because I know I've said a lot.
How does this compare to what you're currently doing?

I think that's… I mean… I mean, I know when I'm on Facebook or Instagram, I see those ads come through, and it's… it's using probably whatever… I guess where I… what I'm typing into my search engine, whatever information's collected, so I… I would think that's probably pretty accurate, accurate, you know, when someone's looking for an apartment.
you know, I think that's probably gonna target… who we want to target using that.
I mean, this is… this is interesting, for sure.
Yeah.
Yeah, I mean, it's all been huge,
Thank you.
So, like, with everything, you get everything we talked about.
Yeah.
one success story… well, I've already kind of told you about S2, Sunridge, Indus, and all of them.
We put one in here specifically, it's Cornerstone.
They have properties all over the Midwest and Mountain West.
Hmm.
But… almost an identical scenario to what you were talking about, right?
Apartments.
com, paid search on Google, that was their primary focuses.
They felt like they were paying a lot of money on Apartments.
com and not getting… not getting results.
When they brought us on in 2023, we were their best advertising source across the board by providing the most leases and the lowest cost per lead of less than $4.
Mom, okay.
And they've just renewed for all of 2026 as well.
So….
Well, let's go.
Yeah.
Are you gonna have this slideshow for me, so I can show this to some of my regionals, or…?
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
I can send everything over that we've talked about, bullets, pricing, as soon as we hop off.
Okay.
So, and then when it comes to pricing, … We do require a 5-property minimum when somebody starts on with us.
Okay.
But our price is only $199 a month per property.
Minimum.
Right.
Okay.
And that gets you our rentable network, our syndicate partnerships, our paid social, a dedicated success manager, access to our backend dashboard as well.
It gets you everything.
Okay.
I mean, that's nothing from what we're paying for leads on, that I'm only seeing on Apartments.
com that have generated one, so….
Yeah, yeah, that's heroes.
I'd be interested.
How long are the contract terms?
We normally do annuals, but right now we're running as short as 6 months.
And that's the lowest they let me go, is 6 months.
Okay.
And then I'll show you this as well, just because I think this might interest you.
I don't know how often and how much you're into looking into, like, data and analytics and whatnot.
But in terms of, like, our backend dashboard.
You'll log in, like, originally what you see is a listing here, and it shows, like, all the properties you have on.
If you go to.
Am I?
Here, it'll show it auto-sets to the past 30 days, so we've sent 448 leads in the past 30 days, and it shows you how many were sent to whichever properties.
That's name.
And then you can change the date range as well, so you can go past… back to the past 90 days.
And you can see in the past 90 days, we've sent, this is S2 Residential out of Dallas, 2,086 leads in the past 90 days.
Hmm.
How many were emails, how many were calls, total leads for each property.
You can also go in and look at what the leads were.
So you can see today, at 3.
56 AM, somebody applied to Oxford Apartments.
Here's what they said and what they asked.
You can go into calls as well.
You can see on 721, there was a call.
Felix Apartments, this call was recorded, so you can go in and listen to exactly what that call sounded like.
Yeah.
So… and then.
Okay.
I had a question.
Yeah, go for it.
So I would have access to the analytics Yup.
A quarter, so I can go in there and see exactly what's going on.
Yep, you, you will.

You can have access to all the properties, and then, like, you have different regionals, obviously, so we can even give them access as well, and we can limit it to where they're only seeing their individual portfolios.
Okay.
So, give as many people access as you want, we don't charge per user seat.
Okay, that's great.
So would you say mainly the leads are coming off of social media?
I'd say it's a mixture of everything, honestly.
Our site gets a lot of traffic in itself, just because, like, for our site in particular, we pick up on the longer keyword searches a lot better than some other sites.
So, like, if somebody goes online and says, you know, I'm looking for a two-bedroom apartment under X amount of dollars that's pet-friendly, like, we're gonna be one of the top sites that show up.
on the top, isn't that crazy, how they're always on top?
Apartments.
com is always on top, because the first thing you see is a sponsored ad.
Oh, well, yeah.
But yeah, so Apartments.
com is always gonna be number one, and that's just… I mean, they're putting a billion dollars a year into advertising and ads and everything else, so, I mean, it's hard to compete with that, but….
Yeah.
A lot of people now have sites that they go to, they've liked the… the… how user-friendly they are, and they repeat it.
again.
We go to Google, so….
like, seeing how, like, I think what you might have on them is the social media part, that's why I was asking about that, because…
I think that would be another alternate way to kind of bypass the fact that they mainly have Google, but obviously, being on top is not doing anything.
Yeah.
Well, it's because you're competing with everybody else that's also paying $2,000 or more a month to….
Right.
To get higher on their listings, and… Yeah, I mean, unless you're wanting to spend $5,000 a month, you're not gonna be on page one.
Right?
So, but yeah, our social ads do generate a lot of traffic, and a lot of leads, so… so it's definitely a big… attribute that we have.

Yeah, and, some of our advertising I guess, … Vendors that we've hired, marketing, … They've actually been pretty successful with social media on that side, as far as the advertising, and a lot of leads coming from that.
So… I would like to get, get, find a company that's more active on the social media.
We are active on there, to say the least.
Yeah, yeah.
We've been a partner with Meta for years.
Our old… our old way that we would do it was through Facebook Marketplace.
Yeah, and that's good.
attitude.

That's popular, too, especially, I guess, with, you know, some of our properties, I guess English is not their first language, and it seems like Facebook Marketplace might be more of a… A way they serve… a tool for them, also.
sometimes… so, Meta changed their policy at the beginning of 23 to eliminate companies being able to.
put an automatic feed into Facebook Marketplace, so we can't do that anymore.
But our social ad piece, making it all included, was, like, our workaround.

And then all of our listings, like, whether it goes to a syndicate site or to Facebook or Instagram, right, they're all translatable, so if somebody has their settings on their phone in Spanish, it'll show and read in Spanish.
Oh, okay.
Yeah.
That's… that's interesting, too.
Didn't think about that.
But you're right.
I've been handed the phone, and it's in Spanish, so I didn't…
Alright.
I mean, that's it, I guess, huh?
That's… yes, sorry, that's rentable.
So I guess, like, in terms of… For you, you said you needed to talk with some regionals.
Yeah, I'd like to have your slideshow, and I can see who's interested in it, and try to grab 5 properties, maybe for a trial.
And if that's successful, then we might… Add more.
Yeah, no, that works for me.
Should we lose management in a property?
Obviously, the contract would be voided, right?
Just want to make sure.
Yeah, I'll make a… I'll put a clause in there, it's like a 30-day out props holder change of management.
All you have to do is provide us proof, and then we let it out.
Yeah.
Okay.
Making a note here so I don't forget that part.
But yeah, I guess in terms of you discussing everything with regionals, when would be a good day for us to follow up?
It may… well, I'm gonna be… Traveling next week.
So, … I'd probably say… Thursday or Friday of next week, if you want to follow up and see where we're at.
Okay, like the 28th Right.
Yeah, probably the 29th, actually, probably would be better, Friday.
That's fine.
If not, that following Monday.
Let's do that following Monday.
Give me some time to kind of talk to them.
Yeah, no, that works.
So, we'll do the… I'll try the 29th, and if… If we have nothing by then, we'll do the first.
Okay.
Do you want to do everything over a Zoom call again, or do you want to just do it through email?
Oh, email's fine, yeah.
Cool.
Yeah.
I'll send you a follow-up email with all the information we just discussed, and… Let me know if you have any questions.
All right, thank you, Nathan.
You're welcome, Jessica, it was great meeting you.
Nice meeting you too, thank you.
Bye.
